# Mode-6 Forbidden Phrases (auto-generated)

**Do not edit this file by hand.** Edit `shared/taxonomies/forbidden_phrases.py`,
then run `python3 -m shared.taxonomies.forbidden_phrases --emit-md > stages/stage-2-write/forbidden_phrases.md`.

Manuscript text containing any of these phrases (case-insensitive,
excluding references and bibliography sections) triggers Stage 2/2.5
FAIL: either methodology-fabrication (subjunctive 'would show' tells) or
internal-provenance leakage (run-harness jargon, artifact-citation tags).

Bracket rule (the class behind the tag entries below): the ONLY bracketed
marker permitted in a manuscript body is a literature citation `[refN]`.
Any other bracketed token (`[A1]`, `[S1]`, `[artifact_ref:...]`, ...) is a leak.

- `would list`
- `would report`
- `would show`
- `would contain`
- `would include`
- `would describe`
- `intended table`
- `intended structure`
- `intended figure`
- `in the planned`
- `in the intended`
- `planned analysis`
- `envisioned table`
- `envisioned analysis`
- `if the data were`
- `if we had`
- `had data been available`
- `table would list`
- `figure would show`
- `such a table would`
- `executed run`
- `executed summary`
- `executed package`
- `the executed design`
- `local research package`
- `research-package archive`
- `research package archive`
- `generated figure metadata`
- `the result omits`
- `the result omitted`
- `local synthetic experiment`
- `artifact citations`
- `[artifact_ref:`
- `[evidence_ref:`
- `[results_csv]`
- `[summary_json]`
- `[current_report_md]`
- `[n_metric_`
